name: Cicaplast Baume B5+
hero: The post-laser balm derms hand out by the box. Panthenol-5%, madecassoside, occlusive shea base. It has nothing exciting in it — and that, after a tretinoin overshoot or a sunburn, is the entire point.

scoreBreakdown.n: Pantothenic acid 5% + madecassoside in occlusive base. Excellent vehicle.
Multiple post-procedure RCTs; barrier-repair endpoints replicated.
Fragrance-free, dye-free, paraben-free. Pediatric and adult tolerance excellent.
₹1,400 / 100 mL is fair internationally; expensive against Indian alternates.

ingredients.note: Helps lock in repair without pore-blocking risk.

alts.name: Derma Repair Balm
alts.note: Same active (panthenol 5%), drugstore price, no madecassoside.

alts.name: Healing Ointment
alts.note: Petroleum-base alternative. Heavier feel, equal occlusion.

alts.name: Healing Ointment
alts.note: The American post-procedure default. Petrolatum dominant.

faq.q: Is it really an everyday moisturiser?
faq.a: Not for everyone. Cicaplast is an occlusive recovery balm — outstanding overnight on compromised skin, post-laser, post-acid burn. As a daily AM moisturiser it's heavy and unnecessary unless your barrier is genuinely compromised.

faq.q: Cicaplast or Cicaplast B5 Hyalu? What's the difference?
faq.a: B5 Baume is the original occlusive. The newer Hyalu B5 serum is a hydrator-only product without the panthenol-occlusive complex. They serve different roles — buy the Baume.

faq.q: Can I use it on babies?
faq.a: Yes — it's pediatric-rated by LRP. Diaper rash, eczema flares, post-vaccine sites. The fragrance-free formula is one of the few balms we recommend without caveats for infant skin.
On non-acne-prone skin, no. On active acne it can occlude existing comedones — apply only to dry, peeled, or post-procedure patches, not the full face.
